purpose: V5-MOD-1's adversary BLOCKING perf-gate (per v5-roadmap.md §Adversary verdict) requires that scoring round-trip latency stay within 5% of this baseline. V5-FOUND-1 captures the baseline; V5-MOD-1's dyn-dispatch implementation reads it and fails-loud if regressed.

captured_against.host: Windows 11 Home, MSI laptop, 8 GB RAM (representative dev workstation)
captured_against.git_sha_baseline_short_note: Phase 4 commit; Phase 5's context.rs instrumentation was applied to the working tree at capture time but the scoring path itself (build_scorecard → build_agent_output) is bit-identical to what e518708 committed.
captured_against.build_profile: debug (cargo build, dev profile, unoptimized + debuginfo)

stage_context.v5_entry_status: pinned 2026-05-01 (concurrent with in-flight v4.x S15/S16 work, per operator pin recorded in v5-roadmap.md §v5 Entry Decision Tracker)
stage_context.s16_status: v4.5 S16-M-1 iter 1 in flight (Plumbing page)
stage_context.pre_s15_ship_note: This baseline is captured pre-S15-ship. The v5 gate-lift commit (NeuroGrim 1378915) explicitly noted: 'if S15 ships scoring-path-affecting changes before V5-MOD-1 runs, the baseline must be re-captured.' The recapture-trigger files are listed under recapture_required_if_changes_to below.

capture_protocol.warmup_treatment: ledger cleared after warmup; warmup runs are NOT in the measured distribution

distribution_ms.name: score.pipeline.run
distribution_ms.min_ms_observed: (not exported by diag report v1; tightness inferred from p50=16 → max=18 — a 2 ms total spread)

v5_mod_1_perf_gate.rule: V5-MOD-1's dyn-dispatch implementation MUST keep scoring round-trip p95_ms ≤ 5% above this baseline's p95_ms.
v5_mod_1_perf_gate.comparison_protocol: Re-run the same capture protocol against the V5-MOD-1 branch (same build_profile + same registry + same host class). If the new p95_ms exceeds 19 ms, V5-MOD-1 fails the perf-gate; revisit dispatch pattern (generic-bounded vs `dyn`) before continuing Theme B — per v5-roadmap.md §Adversary verdict (🔴 Blocking concerns).

caveats: DEBUG BUILD: numbers are higher than a release build would produce (Rust debug typically 2-5x slower than release on hot paths). A future release-build baseline (likely captured at v5 close-out) gives more representative production numbers; the V5-MOD-1 comparison is valid as long as both baseline and target use the SAME build profile.
SINGLE HOST: captured on one Windows 11 / MSI laptop. Cross-platform variance not characterized. V5-MOD-1's perf comparison should run on the same host class to factor out hardware noise.

TIGHT DISTRIBUTION: 30 runs spread only 2 ms (16-18); the workload is small (NeuroGrim's own registry, ~19 domains) and the scoring path is currently fast. Larger registries (e.g., LaaS at 16 domains + heavy CMDBs) could shift the distribution; treat this baseline as the lower bound on acceptable latency.
